sourceSummary.oneLine: Accreditation scope No. KT155 lists electrical testing methods, product/material categories, standards, test ranges, sites, and field-testing status.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as evidence of listed testing scope for electrical safety, radio/wireless, medical electrical equipment, EMC, and energy efficiency standards.
keyPoints: The document identifies scope No. KT155 across pages 2/297 through 297/297.
Electrical Testing scope includes measuring instruments, household electrical machinery, wired/wireless communication devices, medical devices, EMC, and energy efficiency.
For medical devices, the scope includes IEC/EN 60601 and IEC/EN/ISO 80601 medical electrical equipment standards, including diagnostic X-ray equipment standards.
For EMC, the scope includes CISPR, EN, IEC, ETSI, FCC/CFR, SANS, and related EMC test standards.
For energy efficiency, the scope includes 10 CFR Part 430 appendices, EPA Energy Star Program Requirements, IEC 62552, IEC 62301, and Korean MOTIE notices.

evidence.claim: Scope No. KT155 includes electrical testing for measuring instruments.
The measuring instruments scope includes IP Code enclosure protection testing under EN 60529 and IEC 60529.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60529:1991 /A1:2000 /A2:2013 Measuring instruments Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code)

evidence.claim: The scope includes household and similar electrical appliance safety testing under EN 60335 and IEC 60335 series standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Household and similar electrical appliances – Safety – Part 1: General requirements

evidence.claim: The wired/wireless communication devices scope includes ANSI C63.10 compliance testing of unlicensed wireless devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ANSI C63.10 - 2013 Wired/wireless communication devices American National Standard of Procedures for Compliance Testing of Unlicensed Wireless Devices

evidence.claim: The wired/wireless communication devices scope includes FCC radio frequency device standards.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC Part 15 Subpart C:2021 Wired/wireless communication devices RADIO FREQUENCY DEVICES ; Intentional Radiators

evidence.claim: The medical devices scope includes EN 60601-1 general requirements for basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1:2006 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment– Part 1: General requirement for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: The medical devices scope includes diagnostic X-ray radiation protection under EN 60601-1-3.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-3:2008 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment– Part 1-3: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance– Collateral Standard: Radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment

evidence.claim: The medical devices scope includes X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy under EN 60601-2-54 and IEC 60601-2-54.
evidence.evidenceText: Part 2-54: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy

evidence.claim: The EMC scope includes medical electrical equipment EMC under EN 60601-1-2.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-2:2015 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment – Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Electromagnetic disturbances - Requirements and tests

evidence.claim: The EMC scope includes CFR 47 Part 15 for telecommunication radio frequency devices.
evidence.evidenceText: CFR 47 Part 15 Wired/wireless communication devices Telecommunication-radio frequency devices

evidence.claim: The EMC scope includes ETSI EN 301 489 radio equipment EMC standards.
evidence.evidenceText: ETSI EN 301 489-1 V2.2.3(2019-11) Wired/wireless communication devices ElectroMagnetic Compatibility (EMC) standard for radio equipment and services

evidence.claim: The energy efficiency scope includes 10 CFR Part 430 test methods for refrigerators, television sets, battery chargers, and external power supplies.
evidence.evidenceText: 10 CFR Part 430, Subpart B, Appendix A Uniform Test Method for Measuring the Energy Consumption of Refrigerators, Refrigerator-Freezers, and Miscellaneous Refrigeration Products
evidence.pageTarget: 10 CFR Part 430 energy efficiency test methods

evidence.claim: The energy efficiency scope includes EPA Energy Star Program Requirements for audio/video, computers, televisions, displays, and external power supplies.
evidence.evidenceText: EPA Energy Star Program Requirements ... for Audio/Video Version 3.0 ... for ComputersVersion 9.0 ... for TelevisionsVersion 9.0 ... for DisplaysVersion 8.0 ... for single Voltage External Ac-Dc and Ac-Ac Power supplies Version 2.0

evidence.claim: The energy efficiency scope includes Korean MOTIE notices for standby power reduction and energy efficiency labeling and standards.
evidence.evidenceText: MOTIE Notice No. 2022-33호 (2022.2.15.) Regulation on Standby Power Reduction Program

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not explicitly present in the source text; the filename contains 20231110 but this was not used as documentDate.
Revision or version for the overall document is not present in the source text.
Authority signal says FDA, but the source text does not mention FDA and instead lists FCC, CFR, ETSI/EU, MOTIE, and other standards bodies.
Product models are not identified; the source text lists product/material categories and standards rather than specific product models.
Some OCR artifacts and typographical errors are visible, such as 'Ddoor', 'hazarous', 'subjet', and inconsistent unit rendering.
